'''Tristan Powell''' (born 9 February 2001) is an [[Australia]]n professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Betfred Super League]].

He has previously played for the [[Melbourne Storm]] in the [[National Rugby League]]. Powell was contracted to the [[Gold Coast Titans]] and the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in the [[NRL]]. He also played for the [[Brisbane Tigers]], as well as the [[Burleigh Bears]] in two separate spells in the [[Queensland Cup]].<ref>[https://www.itsrugby.co.uk/players/tristan-powell-48606.html itsrugby profile]</ref>

==Background==
Powell was born in [[Hervey Bay, Queensland]], Australia.<ref>[https://www.zerotackle.com/players/tristan-powell/ Tristan Powell]</ref>

He played for the Goondiwindi Boars as a junior. He played SEQ White at under 16 level in the 2017 QRL State Championship. At under 18 level he played for the [[Norths Devils]] in the 2018 [[Mal Meninga Cup]]. The following season he moved to the [[Burleigh Bears]], playing in the 2019 Mal Meninga Cup. He played for the [[Burleigh Bears|Burleigh]] Colts side between 2019 and 2021.<ref>[https://18thman.com/players/tristan-powell/ Tristan Powell]</ref>

Powell represented [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]] at both under-16 and under-18 level.<ref>[https://www.zerotackle.com/ex-storm-forward-joins-new-nrl-team-216639/ Ex-Storm forward joins new NRL team]</ref><ref>[https://www.nrl.com/players/state-of-origin-u18s/qld/tristan-powell/ Tristan Powell]</ref>

==Career==
Powell was a development player for the [[Gold Coast Titans]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]], joining the club on trial in 2019 and leaving the club at the end of the [[2021 NRL season]].<ref>[https://www.thechronicle.com.au/subscribe/news/1/?sourceCode=TCWEB_WRE170_a_GGL&dest=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.thechronicle.com.au%2Fsport%2Fpowell-to-train-with-nrl-squad%2Fnews-story%2F43c8a09fca34c3e8d38b6b472faa14e4&memtype=anonymous&mode=premium Powell to train with NRL squad]</ref><ref>[https://www.titans.com.au/news/2021/09/14/titans-thank-departing-players/ Titans thank departing players]</ref>

He made his debut in April 2021 for the [[Burleigh Bears]] against the [[Tweed Heads Seagulls]] in the [[2021 Queensland Cup|Queensland Cup]]. He played 19 reserve grade games for the [[Burleigh Bears|Bears]] between 2021 and 2022.

Powell was contracted on the development list by the [[Melbourne Storm]] in the NRL in 2023, and played for the [[Brisbane Tigers]], the feeder side of the [[Melbourne Storm|Storm]] in the 2023 and 2024 [[QLD Cup]] seasons.<ref>[https://www.melbournestorm.com.au/news/2022/11/25/meet-our-2023-squad/ Meet our 2023 squad]</ref> 

He earned the [[Brisbane Tigers|Tigers]] forward of the year honour in 2023.<ref>[https://www.melbournestorm.com.au/news/2023/10/26/season-in-review-tristan-powell/ Season in Review: Tristan Powell]</ref> He won the [[2023 Queensland Cup]] with victory of his former side Burleigh.<ref>[https://www.qrl.com.au/draw/qrl-premiership/2023/grand-final/bears-v-tigers/ Bears 18-22 Tigers]</ref>

In August 2024 Powell made his [[Melbourne Storm|Melbourne]] first-grade debut in the National Rugby League against the [[North Queensland Cowboys]].<ref>[https://www.totalrl.com/huddersfield-giants-sign-australian-prop/ Huddersfield Giants sign Australian prop]</ref>

Ahead of the [[2025 NRL season]] he returned to the [[Burleigh Bears]], as a contracted player with the [[Brisbane Broncos]].<ref>[https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2024-every-clubs-trainandtrial-players-latrell-siegwalt-broncos-morgan-harper-storm-players-on-the-rise-nrl-rosters/news-story/4136a3f36efccfbe47115aabe60e373b Sons of guns, schoolboys sensations and code switchers: Every NRL club’s train-and-trial lists]</ref>

Powell joined the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]] on a two-and-a-half year deal in May 2025.<ref>[https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/huddersfield-giants-strengthen-pack-with-signing-of-australian-forward-5140901 Huddersfield Giants strengthen pack with signing of Australian forward]</ref>
